description:
Your role: You are a FinOps analyst who just received two months of cloud-usage CSV files exported from the billing console of a distributed system.  
        Goal: Produce a simple text report that shows (a) the grand total cost and (b) the subtotal cost of each cloud service across both months.

        1. Source data  
           • Directory that already exists: /home/user/cloud_logs/  
           • Files and guaranteed contents (comma-separated values, header in first row):

             /home/user/cloud_logs/usage_jan.csv
             ─────────────────────────────────────
             date,service,resource,cost
             2023-01-05,Compute,vm-1,10.50
             2023-01-10,Storage,s3-bucket-1,2.75
             2023-01-15,Compute,vm-2,7.25

             /home/user/cloud_logs/usage_feb.csv
             ─────────────────────────────────────
             date,service,resource,cost
             2023-02-03,Compute,vm-1,9.00
             2023-02-08,Storage,s3-bucket-1,2.75

        2. What you must create  
           • Make a new directory: /home/user/finops_report/  
           • Inside it create a single ASCII text file named summary.log

        3. Exact format that the automated test will validate  
           • The file must contain EXACTLY four non-empty lines (use Unix LF line endings):

             Line 1: TOTAL_COST:<grand_total_value_with_two_decimals>
             Line 2: SERVICE_BREAKDOWN:
             Line 3: Compute <compute_subtotal_with_two_decimals>
             Line 4: Storage <storage_subtotal_with_two_decimals>

           • Do NOT include extra spaces before “TOTAL_COST” or “SERVICE_BREAKDOWN”.  
           • There must be exactly one space between each service name and its subtotal.  
           • All monetary numbers must always show two digits after the decimal point (e.g. 26.75, not 26.7).

        4. Constraints  
           • Finish the task using no more than about 3-4 standard shell commands (e.g., mkdir, awk, sort, redirection, etc.).  
           • Do everything as the normal user; no sudo is available.  
           • Make sure summary.log is readable (default user perms are fine).  

        5. How the grader checks your work  
           • It will read /home/user/finops_report/summary.log  
           • It will compare its four lines and their numerical values to the format & totals described above.  
           • Any deviation in spelling, extra whitespace, extra lines, or wrong amounts will fail the check.

        Once summary.log is present and formatted correctly, the task is complete.
truth:
Expected file after successful completion:
        ──────────────────────────────────────────
        Path: /home/user/finops_report/summary.log

        Contents (4 lines, Unix LF):
        TOTAL_COST:32.25
        SERVICE_BREAKDOWN:
        Compute 26.75
        Storage 5.50
